title: Writing fractions as repeating decimals
description: Practice writing fractions as repeating decimals. Get ready to bust out those long division skills!

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select the decimal that is equivalent to $\dfrac{19}{27}$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A rational number in the form of a fraction can be represented as a decimal by dividing the numerator by the denominator.   For example, this is how we get $0.75=\dfrac{\pink{3}}{\blue{4}}$ :
                      
  $ \hphantom{4|3}  .75\\
{\blue4}|\overline{\pink{3.00}}\\
\hphantom{4|}\llap{-}\underline{2.8}\\
\hphantom{4|3.}20\\
\hphantom{4|3.}\llap{-}\underline{20}\\
\hphantom{4|3.7}0$
Now let's divide $\pink{19.000000}...$ by $\blue{27}$.  Find enough decimal digits in the numerator so we can see whether the decimal terminates or starts to repeat. 


  $ \hphantom{37|29}  .703703...\\
{\blue{27}}|\overline{\pink{19.000000}...}\\
\hphantom{37|}\llap{-}\underline{189}\\
\hphantom{37|3..}10\\
\hphantom{37|3.}\underline{-0}\\
\hphantom{37|3..}100\\
\hphantom{37|3....}\llap{-}\underline{81}\\
\hphantom{37|3....}190\\
\hphantom{37|3....}\llap{-}\underline{189}\\
\hphantom{37|3.......}10\\
\hphantom{37|3......}\underline{-0}\\
\hphantom{37|3.......}100\\
\hphantom{37|3.........}\llap{-}\underline{81}\\
\hphantom{37|3.........}190...\\
$

Since the number $190$ repeats itself, this is a repeating decimal.

The repeating decimal is $\dfrac{\pink{19}}{\blue{27}} = 0.703703... = 0.\overline{703}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select the decimal that is equivalent to $\dfrac{29}{30}$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A rational number in the form of a fraction can be represented as a decimal by dividing the numerator by the denominator.   For example, this is how we get $0.75=\dfrac{\pink{3}}{\blue{4}}$ :
                      
  $ \hphantom{4|3}  .75\\
{\blue4}|\overline{\pink{3.00}}\\
\hphantom{4|}\llap{-}\underline{2.8}\\
\hphantom{4|3.}20\\
\hphantom{4|3.}\llap{-}\underline{20}\\
\hphantom{4|3.7}0$

Now let's divide $\pink{29.0000}...$ by $\blue{30}$.  Find enough decimal digits in the numerator so we can see whether the decimal terminates or starts to repeat. 


  $ \hphantom{37|29}  .9666...\\
{\blue{30}}|\overline{\pink{29.0000}...}\\
\hphantom{37|}\llap{-}\underline{270}\\
\hphantom{37|3}200\\
\hphantom{37|3}\llap{-}\underline{180}\\
\hphantom{37|3..}200\\
\hphantom{37|3..}\llap{-}\underline{180}\\
\hphantom{37|3....}200\\
\hphantom{37|3....}\llap{-}\underline{180}\\
\hphantom{37|3......}200...\\
$

Since the number $200$ repeats itself, this is a repeating decimal.
The repeating decimal is $\dfrac{\pink{29}}{\blue{30}} = 0.9666... = 0.9\overline{6}$.

**Select the decimal that is equivalent to $\dfrac{63}{75}$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A rational number in the form of a fraction can be represented as a decimal by dividing the numerator by the denominator.   For example, this is how we get $0.75=\dfrac{\pink{3}}{\blue{4}}$ :
                      
  $ \hphantom{4|3}  .75\\
{\blue4}|\overline{\pink{3.00}}\\
\hphantom{4|}\llap{-}\underline{2.8}\\
\hphantom{4|3.}20\\

  $ \hphantom{75|63}  .84\\

The terminating decimal is $\dfrac{\pink{63}}{\blue{75}} = 0.84$.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A rational number in the form of a fraction can be represented as a decimal by dividing the numerator by the denominator.   For example, this is how we get $0.75=\dfrac{\pink{3}}{\blue{4}}$ :

\hphantom{4|}\llap{-}\underline{2.8}\\

\hphantom{4|3.7}0$
Now let's divide $\pink{101.000000}...$ by $\blue{500}$.  Find enough decimal digits in the numerator so we can see whether the decimal terminates or starts to repeat. 


  $ \hphantom{1111|67}  .2020\\
{\blue{500}}|\overline{\pink{101.000000}...}\\
\hphantom{111|}\llap{-}\underline{1000}\\
\hphantom{1111|}100\\
\hphantom{1111.....}\llap{-}\underline{0}\\
\hphantom{11111|..}1000\\
\hphantom{11111|6.}\llap{-}\underline{1000}\\
\hphantom{111111|67...}0\\
$

Since the remainder is $0$, this is a terminating decimal.

The terminating decimal is $\dfrac{\pink{101}}{\blue{500}}  = 0.202$.
